title: Pilosella euryanthela
intro: Pilosella euryanthela, also known as the Eurasian hawkweed or yellow hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, digestive improvement, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella euryanthela include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pilosella euryanthela include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Pilosella euryanthela is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants commonly known as false dandelions. Within this genus, Pilosella euryanthela is considered a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and features. The classification of Pilosella euryanthela is based on its morphology, genetics, and phylogenetic relationships with other related species. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary history and diversity of the Pilosella genus.
